search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae laboratory monitoring chronic conditions telehealth guideline PDF
site:dha.gov.ae lab result review telehealth guideline UAE
site:nice.org.uk laboratory result review clinician guidance
candidate_sources_rejected: condition-specific monitoring tables as universal lab-review rules
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Condition-specific monitoring cannot support every laboratory-review encounter.
Automatic urgency or interpretation is outside documentation scope.
Commercial interpretation pages are not authoritative clinical guidance.
population_applicability: Partial: the DHA document covers laboratory monitoring in named chronic conditions; the workflow is a broader clinician-led result-review template.
setting_applicability: Direct for DHA-licensed virtual chronic-condition monitoring and partial for general outpatient laboratory review.
UAE_applicability: Verified for Dubai Health Authority telehealth monitoring. Result interpretation and action remain clinician responsibilities.
recency_verification: The current DHA Issue 2 laboratory-monitoring PDF, due for revision 2029-02-21, was reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: No newer official DHA laboratory-monitoring issue was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports clinician-led laboratory monitoring and medicine-change context for chronic-condition virtual review.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines medication and report verification, DHA telehealth scope, emergency exclusions, and laboratory-monitoring purpose.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports documenting reviewed laboratory results and comparison with previous monitoring results without interpreting them.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists condition-linked baseline and follow-up laboratory monitoring and states that unlisted testing remains clinician-directed.

unresolved_source_gaps: Broad result-review aliases, reason for testing, current symptoms, vitals, critical or significantly abnormal results, result interpretation, explanation, further tests, referral, return advice, pending results, fixed follow-up, and the combined history draft remain unsupported.
The workflow must not interpret a result, infer urgency, or suggest management unless the clinician documents it.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.

legacy_item_support_mappings.direct_relationship: The exact scope supports a clinician-led laboratory-monitoring visit and medicine-change context without autonomous interpretation.
